notes: Acquired by SAP for $5.8 billion in July 2010. SAP retired the Sybase brand in 2014. Sybase SQL Server technology lineage lives on in SAP ASE.

Sold database subsidiary to IBM for $1 billion in April 2001 after 1997 accounting fraud scandal. Renamed Ascential Software; Ascential acquired by IBM in 2005. Now IBM Informix, active development by HCLSoftware since 2017.
Published this vendor-sponsored profile. Aberdeen Group was acquired by Harte-Hanks in 2001.
Referenced as leading RDBMS competitor. Oracle went on to dominate the enterprise RDBMS market.
Object-relational database company acquired by Informix in 1996; its DataBlade technology became core to Informix Universal Server.
Referenced as major application requiring row-level locking support. SAP later acquired Sybase in 2010.
Acquired by Informix for ROLAP/multidimensional data representation capabilities.
Referenced for network management tool integration with Informix-OnLine 8.0 administrative utilities.
Partner providing spatial DataBlade module for Informix Universal Server.
Partner providing text retrieval DataBlade module for Informix Universal Server.
